title: euphorbia terracina var. obliquata
intro: Euphorbia terracina var. obliquata, also known as the Mediterranean spurge or snake tree, is a succulent plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its milky sap, which contains euphorbon, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Euphorbia terracina var. obliquata include its potential to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as an antiviral agent to combat viral infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture, salve, or ointment, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized for various health benefits.
